input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. Their XAML has `{Binding Title}` and `{Binding Description}`, but the labels appear blank despite the ViewModel having values.

The most common cause: the `BindingContext` is not set. The XAML looks correct syntactically, but without setting the `BindingContext` to an instance of the ViewModel, the bindings have nothing to resolve against.

Another common issue is that the ViewModel properties don't raise `PropertyChanged` notifications (i.e., don't implement `INotifyPropertyChanged`), but the user says "properties have values" which suggests they're set before binding, so the most likely issue is the missing `BindingContext`.


I should provide a quick solution showing how to set the `BindingContext` in the code-behind or XAML, then mention the `INotifyPropertyChanged` fallback if that doesn't work.
